content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum                                                M- 84.150

To     :  Advice Request Meeting Participants          Date  :  May 30, 1984


From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Carla Wardlow

Subject:  California Community Colleges Lobbying Activity


         Several community college organizations pooled their money
and hired a media consultant to produce and mail a package
to the business community which described the benefits of
supporting community colleges. The mailing requested that
the businesses write to the Governor and tell him how
wonderful community colleges are. The return address on
the package used the name "Californians for Community
Colleges."

         The total cost of the mailing was approximately $8,000.
Each contributing college organization will report its
share of the cost on its lobbyist employer report.

         Will "Californians for Community Colleges" be required to
file disclosure statements under Section 86108(b)?


         ANSWER:  No.  Californians for Community Colleges will not
                  be required to file.

State of California

Memorandum

M-84-151

To     : Advice Request Meeting                                    Date  : April 30, 1984


From  : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Carla Wardlow

Subject: Regulation 18423—Personal Services


        1.  We have received a request for some guidelines for determining
whether an employee has spent 10 percent of his or her compensated time on political activities. For example, how would a
corporate executive who has no set working hours determine if
she has spent 10 percent of more of her compensated time on
political activities? Should we use a 40-hour week? Could she
use the average time worked per week for the previous six months
as a guide?

        2.  Section 18423 says that the payment of salary, etc., to an
employee who spends more than 10 percent of his or her time
rendering services for political purposes is a contribution
if:

                (2) The employee, with consent of the employer, is
                relieved of any normal working responsibilities related
                to his employment in order to render the personal services,
                unless the employee engages in political activity on a
                bona fide, although compensable, vacation time, or pursuant to a uniform policy allowing employees to engage
                in political activity.

        What does the underscored language mean?

ANSWER:

        1.  Assume that the individual works a 40-hour week, 9 a.m. to
5 p.m.

        2.  A "uniform policy" means that all employees are allowed to
spend some amount of their compensable time on political
activities of their own choosing.

sections.conclusion: No. Californians for Community Colleges will not be required to file.
sections.question_synthetic: This document contains two separate FPPC advice items: (1) whether a group using the name 'Californians for Community Colleges' must file disclosure statements under Section 86108(b) after conducting an $8,000 lobbying mailing, and (2) how to determine whether an employee has spent 10 percent of compensated time on political activities under Regulation 18423 and what constitutes a 'uniform policy' for political activity.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provided two conclusions: (1) Californians for Community Colleges is not required to file disclosure statements, and (2) for the 10 percent test, assume a standard 40-hour work week and interpret 'uniform policy' to mean that all employees are allowed to spend some compensable time on political activities of their own choosing.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two distinct memoranda merged together without clear section headers. First memo from May 30, 1984 re: lobbying disclosure; second memo from April 30, 1984 re: Regulation 18423 political activity time calculation. Both are FPPC responses with conclusions clearly marked as 'ANSWER:' followed by guidance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: This document contains two separate FPPC advice items: (1) whether a group using the name 'Californians for Community Colleges' must file disclosure statements under Section 86108(b) after conducting an $8,000 lobbying mailing, and (2) how to determine whether an employee has spent 10 percent of compensated time on political activities under Regulation 18423 and what constitutes a 'uniform policy' for political activity.

CONCLUSION: No. Californians for Community Colleges will not be required to file.

The mailing requested that the businesses write to the Governor and tell him how wonderful community colleges are. The return address on the package used the name "Californians for Community Colleges." The total cost of the mailing was approximately $8,000. Each contributing college organization will report its share of the cost on its lobbyist employer report. Will "Californians for Community Colleges" be required to file disclosure statements under Section 86108(b)? ANSWER: No. Californians for Community Colleges will not be required to file. State of California Memorandum M-84-151 To : Advice Request Meeting Date : April 30, 1984 From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Carla Wardlow Subject: Regulation 18423—Personal Services 1. We have received a request for some guidelines for determining whether an employee has spent 10 percent of his or her compensated time on political activities. For example, how would a corporate executive who has no set working hours determine if she has spent 10 percent of more of her compensated time on political activities? Should we use a 40-hour week? Could she use the average time worked per week for the previous six months as a guide? 2. Section 18423 says that the payment of salary, etc., to an employee who spends more than 10 percent of his or her time rendering services for political purposes is a contribution if: (2) The employee, with consent of the employer, is relieved of any normal working responsibilities related to his employment in order to render the personal services, unless the employee engages in political activity on a bona fide, although compensable, vacation time, or pursuant to a uniform policy allowing employees to engage in political activity. What does the underscored language mean? ANSWER: 1. Assume that the individual works a 40-hour week, 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. 2. A "uniform policy" means that all employees are allowed to spend some amount of their compensable time on political activities of their own choosing.
embedding.summary: This FPPC memorandum addresses two separate lobbying and political activity compliance questions from April and May 1984, providing guidance on disclosure filing requirements for community college lobbying efforts and on interpreting the 10 percent political activity threshold under state regulations.
